target: HOL-STRESSTEST c2: dome-defender counter-narrative hardening — falsifiability module tautology critique, hero thesis precision, stance/denominator/living-document moderates
kernel_of_truth: The c2 dome-defender critique has a real methodological observation: the c1 fix (eg-falsifiability section + ds-falsifiability-module) was applied at the prose layer but not the architecture layer. Three of the four falsifiability conditions ARE tightly coupled to the existing verdict criteria (C1 inverse of Std Model Explains; C3 restates Self-Contradicted criterion for the cavity-frequency cluster; C4 restates Misleading criterion for coordinate-system WINs). The four-badge zero stack visually doubles the c1 rigged-framework optics. The dome defender is correct that prose-layer falsifiability is not architecture-layer falsifiability. Likewise the hero thesis overstates: 12 Refuted-by-Data WINs DID produce dome-specific results that differ from standard physics (11.78 Hz, κ=1.67 nT/μGal etc.) — they failed against measurement, not because standard physics already explained the same value. Conflating these two structurally distinct failure modes is exactly the kind of category error the Verdict Assignment Criteria section spends 200 words distinguishing elsewhere. Why it does not save the dome: recognizing the module is rhetorically vulnerable does not validate any verdict. Every individual WIN still fails on either internal consistency, data, or non-discrimination — the architecture-vs-prose gap is a presentation problem, not a verdict problem.
summary_for_decider: 5 structured patches (P1-P5) addressing ISS-2284/2285/2286, plus 3 deferred items (STRESSTEST-DEFER-01/02/03) requiring operator decision. P1 (ISS-2285) reword Overview hero thesis to reflect verdict architecture: 'survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks'. P2 (ISS-2284) add worked example after eg-falsifiability Condition 1 showing what satisfaction would look like (Schumann 22.6 Hz scenario). P3 (ISS-2284) update ds-falsifiability-module caption to emphasize pre-commitment framing + add ~1 nT discriminating-window detail to C2 status row sublabel. P4 (ISS-2286) inline operational definition for 4 silent failures in accuracy-math-section first paragraph. P5 (ISS-2286) reframe Overview stance statement as conclusion-from-evidence rather than starting assumption — resolves the Principle-3-vs-stance tension also flagged in HOL-NARRATIVE c2. Deferred items: DEFER-01 add Genuine-Prediction-pending verdict category (architectural, requires scorecard schema change); DEFER-02 module heading rename What-Would-Change → Operational-Falsifiability-Conditions (rhetorical tradeoff, operator decision); DEFER-03 living-document acknowledgement sentence (opens AI-reviewing-AI attack surface, operator weigh). Patches preserve all verdicts unchanged; this EXP is presentation-layer hardening of the c1 falsifiability framing, not verdict revision.
scope_note: review_class=deep-attack (singleton) because P2 introduces new argumentative prose (worked example template) and P5 touches the review's foundational stance framing. P1, P3, P4 individually would be verification-class but the EXP as a whole is deep-attack scope.

cross_reference_overlap.HOL_NARRATIVE_c2: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 (2026-05-28) independently flagged the stance-statement Principle-3 tension as moderate. P5 closes both this critique and the c2 stress-test version of the same hole. If a separate EXP for HOL-NARRATIVE stance work exists, decider should coordinate to avoid duplicate stance-prose edits.
cross_reference_overlap.HOL_TAXONOMY_c2: HOL-TAXONOMY c2 (2026-05-29) reached the same architectural conclusion via the inside-out lens (Self-Contradicted cluster inflation). The architectural recommendations there overlap with DEFER-01 here; if a verdict-category restructure proposal moves forward, it should be designed to satisfy both review angles.

patches.title: Reword Overview hero thesis to match verdict architecture
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: Current hero conflates Std Model Explains (non-discriminating; dome value = standard value) with Refuted by Data (discriminating; dome value != standard value, dome wrong) — two structurally distinct failure modes. The Verdict Assignment Criteria section devotes ~200 words to this exact distinction; the hero ignores it. 12 Refuted-by-Data WINs (WIN-001 11.78 Hz, WIN-070 κ=1.67 nT/μGal, etc.) DID produce dome-specific results standard physics does not predict. The honest formulation describes WHY they fail (internal-consistency OR measurement), not whether they fail to discriminate at all.
patches.find_text: None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.
patches.replace_text: None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks.

sublabel_change.find_text: None produces a dome-derived prediction that both differs from standard physics and is confirmed by measurement. What would change this?
sublabel_change.replace_text: None produces a dome-derived prediction that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks. What would change this?

patches.effect: Hero now matches the verdict-bar architecture below it. A dome defender attempting the c2 hero-contradicts-verdicts attack must now point to a WIN that produces a dome-specific result AND survives both gates — which is exactly the Confirmed category that does not exist because no such WIN exists. The rewording closes the conflation without weakening the rhetorical force (0 still survive scrutiny).
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: It is still a sweeping claim. Counter: yes, but it is a sweeping claim that matches what our verdict architecture actually finds. The previous wording made a sweeping claim that the verdict architecture itself contradicted (12 Refuted-by-Data WINs are dome-specific by construction). This wording is sweeping in a way the architecture supports.

patches.title: Add worked-example paragraph after eg-falsifiability Condition 1
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: The tautology critique lands rhetorically because no template exists for what Condition-1 satisfaction would look like. A reader sees four conditions, four zeros, and concludes the conditions cannot be met. A concrete fictional scenario showing what satisfaction looks like (and why the actual evidence does not meet it) breaks the appearance of fixed-frame restatement. The Schumann case is the cleanest illustrative example: the dome cavity, on its own geometry, predicts 22-35 Hz; if observed Schumann were 22.6 Hz and the dome had derived 22.6 Hz from disc radius alone before measurement, that would be Condition-1 satisfaction. The reason it does not satisfy is that observed Schumann is 7.83 Hz and the dome can only match by importing the globe radius.
patches.target_anchor: eg-falsifiability ps-detail (after Condition 1 paragraph, around line 678)
patches.insert_after_text: WIN-057's best result (6.2% RMSE) is 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-0.01% precision over similar distances (Misleading).
patches.insert_text: What Condition 1 satisfaction would look like (worked example). Suppose the dome cavity, treated as a flat-plate resonator with the published exponentially-decaying firmament profile, predicts a Schumann fundamental of 22.6 Hz from disc radius and firmament height alone — no globe radius input. Suppose further that the dome registered this 22.6 Hz prediction before any Schumann measurement was made, and that independent measurement subsequently returned 22.6 Hz. That sequence would satisfy Condition 1: derived from dome geometry, discriminating from standard physics (which predicts 7.83 Hz from the globe-radius cavity eigenvalue), and confirmed by measurement. We have constructed this scenario to demonstrate the condition is operationalizable — it is not a logically empty test. The reason no actual WIN satisfies it is that the observed Schumann fundamental is 7.83 Hz, and the dome can only reproduce 7.83 Hz by importing the globe radius (6,371 km) into a spherical-cavity eigenvalue formula derived from spherical geometry. The dome cavity on its own dimensions predicts approximately 22–35 Hz, which the data refutes. The conditions are demanding because the dome's own geometry refuses to cooperate, not because the conditions were designed to be unmeetable.
patches.effect: Provides the dome defender with a concrete template for satisfaction, neutralizing the tautology critique with a demonstration that the conditions are operationalizable. Also doubles as positive evidence for the falsifiability framing — we show what we would accept, then show why the dome does not produce it.
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: You picked a deliberately unfavorable scenario. Counter: the scenario uses the dome's own stated geometry. Any alternative scenario would have to either change the dome geometry (which is the dome's problem) or change the observed Schumann frequency (which is the data's problem). Neither is available to the dome defender as a counter.

patches.title: ds-falsifiability-module caption rewrite + C2 status sublabel surfaces discriminating-window detail
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: The module caption frames conditions as assessment results ('assessed against the dome's 70 claimed wins') rather than pre-committed tests. The pre-commitment framing — stronger against the tautology attack — lives in the long-form eg-falsifiability section but is not surfaced in the module itself. Separately, the C2 eclipse status row sublabel reads only 'eclipse Aug 12, 2026' — the discriminating-window detail (~1 nT) that makes C2 a genuine forward test is buried in the long-form section.

sub_patches.find_text: Four conditions assessed against the dome's 70 claimed wins — any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict.
sub_patches.replace_text: Four pre-committed conditions — set before the data, satisfiable by any future dome derivation. We will upgrade the relevant verdict if any one is met.

sub_patches.target_anchor: C2 status row sublabel for genuine prospective prediction (around line 444)
sub_patches.replace_text: 1 partialeclipse Aug 12, 2026 · ~1 nT discriminating window

patches.effect: (P3a) The caption now frames conditions as PRE-COMMITTED tests rather than assessment outputs — this is a rhetorical shift that the tautology critique applies to less cleanly because a pre-commitment is forward-looking by definition. (P3b) The eclipse C2 status sublabel now carries the discriminating-window detail that makes it a real forward test rather than an opaque '1 partial' badge. A casual reader who only scans the module now sees that at least one condition is operationalized to a measurable window.
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: A 1 nT window means the dome's prediction barely differs from standard physics — by your own admission. Counter: yes, and surfacing that admission in the module is a feature, not a bug. The discriminating window is small precisely because the dome scales Chapman-mechanism values; the C2 sublabel now makes that audit-able at-a-glance rather than burying it.

patches.title: Inline operational definition for 4 silent failures in accuracy-math-section
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: The 89.7% honest-accuracy denominator is auditable in the long-form p3-silent-failures section but not on the Overview itself. A dome defender can argue the review constructs its denominator with the same methodological freedom it criticizes the dome for. Inlining a one-clause operational definition closes the audit gap without requiring the reader to click through.
patches.find_text: Computed as 70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from its own accuracy denominator.
patches.replace_text: Computed as 70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has visibly marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED] but excluded from its own accuracy denominator. Each silent-failure entry is named individually with the dome's own removal-action commit referenced in the failure list — the criterion is observable on the dome's public surface, not imputed from absence of result.
patches.effect: The denominator criterion now has an observable definition (dome's own [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] markers) inline on the Overview. The dome defender's symmetric-methodology counter must now argue that quoting the dome's own removal actions is methodologically equivalent to the dome's manually entered 94.5% — which is a much harder argument to make.
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: The dome considers removed entries to be 'refined' not 'failed' — your reading them as failures is interpretive. Counter: the dome's own commit messages call them removals or suspensions, and the dome excludes them from its accuracy denominator. The interpretive question is whether removing-without-counting-as-failure is itself a denominator manipulation; we say it is, and the reader can audit the four named entries to decide independently.

patches.title: Reframe Overview stance statement as conclusion-from-evidence
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: The stance opener 'The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology.' reads as a starting axiom. The Evaluation Guide presents five questions framed as neutral audit criteria. A dome defender can quote the opener as proof that the review assumed its conclusion. The procedural critique lands regardless of whether the conclusion is correct. Reframing the opener as a stated conclusion that the document then defends preserves the rhetorical force (stance is asserted up front) while answering the procedural objection (it is a conclusion, not a premise). Also flagged in HOL-NARRATIVE c2 — patch closes both reviews' version of the hole.
patches.find_text: The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does. The evidence is set out in the twelve tabs below.
patches.replace_text: Our conclusion, stated up front: the world is a globe, and the dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. We arrive at this conclusion from the evidence reviewed in the twelve tabs below, applied through the audit criteria in the Evaluation Guide; we do not assume it as a starting premise. Stating the conclusion in the first paragraph is a courtesy to the reader, not a procedural shortcut.
patches.effect: Closes the Principle-3-vs-stance procedural objection by explicitly framing the opener as a conclusion rather than a premise. Preserves the up-front clarity that operator wanted (the verdict is stated before the tabs begin). Adds the audit-criteria-applied framing that distinguishes a conclusion from an assumption.
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: Stating it in the first paragraph still anchors the reader. Counter: yes, and that is the point of a critical review — we owe the reader our position rather than burying it. The procedural objection was that the opener LOOKED like a premise. Naming it as a conclusion-from-evidence and pointing at the evidence answers the procedural form of the objection without performative neutrality.

deferred_items.addresses: ISS-2284 architecture-level recommendation #2 from curmudgeon Stronger Arguments
deferred_items.title: Add Genuine-Prediction-(pending) verdict category placeholder
deferred_items.why_deferred: Adding a verdict category requires changes to: (a) the verdict-bars architecture (ds-verdict-bars), (b) the per-WIN verdict tag system (vt-* CSS classes), (c) the scorecard counters that compute verdict tallies, (d) the Verdict Assignment Criteria section text, (e) the eg-verdicts 'On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph which would need restating. This is scorecard-schema work beyond per-section expansion scope.
deferred_items.operator_decision_needed: Is the goal to keep the verdict architecture stable until after the August 2026 eclipse (which will materially change the eclipse-tracking entries' status), or to ship the category placeholder now to defeat the tautology critique architecturally?
deferred_items.analyst_recommendation: Defer until the eclipse resolves (mid-August 2026). Reasoning: (a) the placeholder category would currently contain exactly one entry (PRED-eclipse-001), (b) after the eclipse the category either gains entries or empties, (c) shipping the placeholder before the resolution risks the architecture being reworked twice in two months. Worked-example patch P2 above does much of the rhetorical work of demonstrating the conditions are operationalizable without requiring a schema change.

deferred_items.addresses: ISS-2284 architecture-level recommendation #3 from curmudgeon Stronger Arguments
deferred_items.title: Rename ds-falsifiability-module heading: What-Would-Change-Our-Verdict → Operational-Falsifiability-Conditions
deferred_items.why_deferred: Rhetorical tradeoff. 'What would change our verdict' is direct and reader-friendly; 'Operational falsifiability conditions' is more academic and resists the tautology critique better but loses casual-reader accessibility. P3a above (caption rewrite emphasizing pre-commitment) does some of the rhetorical work without losing the direct heading. Operator should weigh the casual-reader vs adversarial-defender axis.
deferred_items.operator_decision_needed: Prioritize casual-reader accessibility (keep current heading) or adversarial-defender resistance (rename)? Worth noting: the heading is the first thing a reader sees in the module; an academic heading reduces engagement.
deferred_items.analyst_recommendation: Keep current heading. P3a's caption rewrite already shifts the framing from assessment to pre-commitment; the heading does not need to do additional work. The casual-reader accessibility of 'What Would Change Our Verdict' is a feature.

deferred_items.title: Add living-document acknowledgment sentence to Overview methodology paragraph
deferred_items.why_deferred: Opens an attack surface (the AI-reviewing-AI tu quoque from c1 secondary rebuttal 2 already exists in the c2 critique surface as the 6-holistic-reviews-in-6-days optic). Explicit acknowledgment may help more than it hurts OR it may make the attack land harder by signaling the review is aware of its own iteration speed. This is a judgment call about whether transparency neutralizes or amplifies the optic.
deferred_items.operator_decision_needed: Is the goal to neutralize the methodology-churn optic by explicit acknowledgment, or to leave it unaddressed on the principle that finding errors in oneself is what adversarial review is supposed to do and casual readers who care about epistemic process will work that out for themselves?
deferred_items.analyst_recommendation: Defer. The optic is logically weak (curmudgeon's purpose is documented in agent prompts and the publicly-visible monitor/ directory); a casual reader who notices the iteration speed will likely also notice the verdict stability (verdicts are not re-litigated; only framing tightens). Explicit acknowledgement adds attack surface without proportionate benefit.

preempt_summary: The composite c2 dome defense (falsifiability-module-restates-verdicts + hero-contradicts-architecture) is closed by P1 (hero rewording matches verdict architecture) + P2 (worked example shows conditions are operationalizable) + P3 (pre-commitment caption frames conditions as forward tests). The stance procedural objection is closed by P5 (conclusion-from-evidence reframing). The denominator-symmetry objection is closed by P4 (inline operational definition). Three architecture-level recommendations (verdict category placeholder, module rename, living-document acknowledgment) deferred pending operator decision; the deferral is not damaging because the prose-layer patches above neutralize the c2 critique without requiring architectural changes.
